MELItem.Title: Passenger Entry Doors Pressure Stop Assemblies

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One pressure stop per door may be inoperative or missing provided:

ListItem.Para: There are no visible defects on remaining stops for the associated door.
Flight is conducted unpressurized.
Procedures are established and used to verify cargo compartments remain empty or contain only ballast, empty cargo handling equipment (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), or fly away kits.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Inspect remaining pressure stops for the associated door and establish airplane in unpressurized flight configuration (AMM DDG 52-11-02).

ListItem.Para: Visually inspect remaining pressure stops for the associated door and fuselage to ensure there are no other defects.

Note.Para: Door stops are numbered 1 to 6 from bottom to top on both forward and aft edges of door.

ListItem.Para: Establish airplane in unpressurized flight configuration.
Open the Outflow valves.
Set the Pressurization Panel (P5) FWD OUTFLOW VALVE and AFT OUTFLOW VALVE switches to MAN.
Hold both manual switches CLOSE until the EICAS outflow valve position indicator reads CL and confirm the forward and aft outflow valve doors are closed.
Hold both manual switches OPEN until the EICAS outflow valve position indicator is in the 1-o’clock position and confirm the forward and aft outflow valve doors are full open.
Leave the Pressurization Panel (P5) FWD OUTFLOW VALVE and AFT OUTFLOW VALVE switches in MAN.
Deactivate the forward and aft lower cargo compartment smoke detection system.
For forward lower cargo compartment smoke detection system, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag.
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 1A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 1B
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- FWD CARGO 2B
For aft lower cargo compartment smoke detection system, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open the following circuit breakers and lock with an INOP tag.
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 1A
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 1B
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 2A
SMOKE DETECTOR- AFT CARGO 2B

Note.Para: The DET FIRE CARGO FWD and DET FIRE CARGO AFT advisory messages and status messages for all cargo fire detectors will be displayed.

Effectivities.Effectivity: For OFCR or OFAR installed (Applicable to: VN-A861, VN-A862, VN-A863, VN-A864, VN-A867, VN-A868, VN-A870, VN-A871):

ListItem.Para: Deactivate the crew/attendant rest(s) closed.
Remove all baggage and personal items from the associated crew/attendant rest.

Note.Para: Blankets, pillows and other items normally used in the crew rest may remain.

ListItem.Para: Close the associated crew/attendant rest entrance door.

DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: One pressure stop per door may be inoperative or missing provided:

ListItem.Para: There are no visible defects on remaining stops for the associated door.
All automatic cabin pressure control channels operate normally.
Airplane pressure altitude does not exceed 12,000 feet.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Visually inspect remaining pressure stops for the associated door and fuselage to ensure there are no other defects (AMM DDG 52-11-02).

Note.Para: Door stops are numbered 1 to 6 from bottom to top on both forward and aft edges of door.

OperationProcedure.Para: Airplane pressure altitude is limited to 12,000 feet.
